name: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address
notes:
This rule triggers when communication with a potential hostile IP address, categorized by X-force or in the reference set collection is detected.

BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (Reference Sets)
   This Building Block defines communication with a potential hostile IP, categorized by reference sets.

   Note: The reference sets starting with "XFE ATPF" prefixes are automatically managed by the Threat Intelligence app. This requires a paid subscription.

   The other reference sets provided by the Threat Intelligence App can be used to include third party Threat Intelligence feeds. Tune these Reference Sets with the adapted Time to Live for each element.


BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (X-force Categorization)
   This Building Block defines communication with a potential hostile IP address, categorized by X-force.
   
   Note: X-force categorization is available for free of use.

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (Reference Sets)
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (X-force Categorization)
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (Reference Sets), BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (X-force Categorization)
TD_list: APPLY Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (Reference Sets), BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address (X-force Categorization)
RED_list: Event name: Communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address
Event description: Detects a communication with a Potential Hostile IP Address, categorized by X-force or in the reference set collection.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
